cold_watch.immediate_felt_reaction: The opening should make a creator recognize editing friction immediately.
cold_watch.moment_of_curiosity: The first caption implies there is a faster way to make the clip usable.
cold_watch.moment_of_confusion_or_boredom: The sample fixture has no real footage, so this remains a low-confidence placeholder observation.
cold_watch.most_memorable_image: Raw footage becoming a polished captioned short.

evidence_timeline.viewer_perception: The viewer recognizes repetitive editing labor.

core_mechanism.name: workflow compression
core_mechanism.why_it_catches_attention: It starts with a familiar editing bottleneck.
core_mechanism.why_it_might_persuade: It promises visible reduction in manual work.
core_mechanism.what_cannot_be_copied: Any competitor wording, visual identity, or exact proof scene.

creative_intent.creative_type: UGC / creator workflow demo / direct response
creative_intent.likely_hypothesis: If creators see raw footage become publishable quickly, they will believe the tool can remove repetitive editing work.
creative_intent.target_audience: Short-form creators and agencies with recurring editing bottlenecks.
creative_intent.likely_kpi: Click-through, sign-up starts, trial activation, and CAC/ROAS.
creative_intent.offer_strategy: Turn an annoying workflow into visible speed proof before asking for the click.
creative_intent.marketer_job_to_be_done: Make the product feel like an output multiplier, not another editing app.
unknowns: No media-buy data or landing-page analytics are available.

distilled_core.core_thesis: The creative turns a familiar editing bottleneck into a visible before-after promise that makes speed feel believable.
distilled_core.mechanism_formula: raw-work pain -> visible transformation -> workflow compression -> action prompt
distilled_core.belief_shift_summary: The viewer moves from 'this clip still needs too much work' to 'this can become publishable with a faster editing system.'
distilled_core.why_it_works: It anchors the promise in a concrete workflow pain before asking the viewer to believe the product claim.
distilled_core.where_it_breaks: The proof weakens if the finished edited output is not shown clearly enough before the viewer loses patience.
distilled_core.submagic_transfer: Show raw footage becoming a captioned, cut, zoomed, publishable short inside the first few seconds.
steal: Open on a creator pain that is already emotionally expensive.
Make the transformation visual before explaining features.
avoid: Do not copy competitor wording, visuals, claims, creator likeness, or music.
Do not let feature lists replace visible before-after proof.
test_next: Test a first-three-seconds before-after edit reveal against a pain-only hook.

core_understanding.human_truth: Creators know raw clips can be valuable but feel trapped by the repetitive labor required to make them publishable.

belief_shift.before: A raw clip still requires too much manual editing to publish consistently.
belief_shift.after: A raw clip can become a polished short quickly when the editing workflow is compressed.
belief_shift.bridge: Visible captions, zooms, cuts, and B-roll turn hidden editing labor into a concrete transformation.

tension_stack: The creator wants higher output volume without lowering perceived quality.
The creator distrusts generic AI editor claims unless the workflow proof is visible.

proof_ladder.claim: Manual editing work can be compressed.
proof_ladder.weakness: The sample fixture needs a clearer finished-video proof frame.

viewer_state_timeline.trigger: The opening frames a familiar editing bottleneck.

core_understanding.skeptic_read: A skeptical creator may reject the promise if the finished output is not shown quickly.
core_understanding.transfer_principle_for_submagic: Turn invisible editing labor into a visible before-after workflow compression demo.
counterfactuals: Showing the final edited clip in the first three seconds would make the promise more concrete.

scores.improvement_note: Real analysis must cite first-frame and first-caption evidence.

drop_off_hypotheses: CTA appears after the likely midpoint retention drop.
risks_and_unknowns: Sample fixture only; no real performance data.
